note: Governs DATIVE with no preposition (ich helfe dem Mann). Also strong/irregular: e->i stem change (hilft), preterite half, participle geholfen. Tests verb-governed case + conjugation overrides.
Fixed prepositional verb: warten auf + AKKUSATIVE. The case is dictated by the verb+preposition pairing, not by motion. Regular conjugation (no overrides).
Movement verb, aux SEIN (not haben). Irregular a->ä (fährt). Used with a TWO-WAY preposition (in) where case depends on motion vs location — that case is a property of the sentence, not the verb.
Separable prefix (ab|fahren): prefix splits to clause end in main clauses (der Zug fährt ab), rejoins in participle (abgefahren). Tests separability + the prefix interacting with conjugation.
